reforms.description: Chile has introduced an R&D tax credit that mandates the involvement of external research institutes, aiming to enhance private sector engagement in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax credit incentivizes private investment in research, fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have taken several measures to improve industry-science relationships.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the innovation policy framework, moving towards a more inclusive approach that encourages private sector participation in R&D.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have taken several measures to improve industry-science relationships to make scientific research more market-relevant.

reforms.description: Chile has enacted a law to simplify bankruptcy procedures for small and medium-sized enterprises, aiming to enhance entrepreneurial risk-taking and access to credit.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying bankruptcy procedures reduces barriers for entrepreneurs, encouraging innovation and the establishment of new businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: Several initiatives to make the bankruptcy law more efficient are under way.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical barrier to entrepreneurship, facilitating the exit of inefficient firms and promoting a healthier startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The government enacted a law that simplifies bankruptcy procedures for small and medium-size enterprises.

reforms.description: Chile is broadening its innovation policy to support all forms of technological and non-technological innovation, moving beyond a narrow focus on basic research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding support for various forms of innovation enhances the overall innovation ecosystem, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Efforts to strengthen links between firms and universities and support all forms of technological and non-technological innovation in firms should be continued.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform signifies a fundamental shift in the approach to innovation policy, aiming to create a more dynamic and responsive innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts to broaden innovation policy beyond basic research should be continued.

reforms.description: Chile's innovation policy framework has shifted focus from favoring basic public research to promoting business innovation, addressing the low rates of technological and non-technological innovation in firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing support for business innovation, the reform aims to increase productivity and competitiveness in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The innovation policy framework has been reformed to better support business innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the innovation policy landscape, aiming to enhance the overall productivity and innovation capacity of the Chilean economy.
reforms.source_quote: Until recently, the innovation policy framework favoured basic public research over business innovation.

reforms.description: The R&D tax incentive introduced in 2008 aims to improve incentives for innovation in the private business sector, encouraging firms to invest in research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing tax incentives, the reform encourages firms to increase their R&D spending, which can lead to greater innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax incentive was introduced to enhance innovation incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the R&D tax incentive represents a significant policy shift aimed at fostering innovation in the private sector, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The R&D tax incentive introduced in 2008 aims at improving incentives for innovation in the private business sector.

reforms.description: The newly created National Innovation Council has started to develop a long-term innovation strategy to guide and enhance innovation efforts across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishing a long-term innovation strategy is expected to provide a coherent framework for fostering innovation, which can lead to improved productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Innovation Council has started to develop a long-term innovation strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the National Innovation Council and its strategy represents a significant step towards a more coordinated approach to innovation policy in Chile.
reforms.source_quote: The newly created National Innovation Council has started to develop a long-term innovation strategy.

reforms.description: The Chilean government introduced an R&D tax credit in 2008, allowing firms to claim a tax credit of 35% for payments made to certified public research institutes for R&D activities, and to deduct 65% of the remaining amount from taxable income.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit aims to incentivize private sector investment in R&D, potentially leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credit was introduced and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the R&D tax credit represents a significant policy change aimed at enhancing private sector engagement in R&D, although its effectiveness remains to be fully evaluated.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have recently taken several measures to promote links between public research institutes or universities and private enterprises. They introduced an R&D tax credit in 2008.

reforms.description: The authorities have launched initiatives to promote links between private enterprises and public research institutes, requiring matching public grants by private funds through the joint Technological Consortia Programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates collaboration between private and public sectors, enhancing technology transfer and innovation capacity.
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant initiative to enhance collaboration between sectors, promoting applied research and technology transfer.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have launched other promising initiatives to promote links between private enterprises and public research institutes that require the matching of public grants by private funds.

reforms.description: The National Innovation Council for Competitiveness was created to set guidelines for a long-term national innovation strategy, separating policy-setting from funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Establishes a coherent framework for innovation policy, enhancing strategic focus and effectiveness in funding allocation.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Innovation Council for Competitiveness was created by decree in 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: A structural change in the innovation policy framework, enhancing coherence and strategic direction for national innovation efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The National Innovation Council for Competitiveness (created by decree in 2006 pending approval by Congress) sets guidelines for long-term national innovation strategy.

reforms.description: The establishment of the National Innovation Council aims to enhance the independence of innovation policy-making by ensuring that its members are not influenced by electoral cycles, thereby promoting a more stable and long-term approach to innovation in Chile.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting stable and independent decision-making in innovation policy, the council can better support long-term investments in R&D and innovation, which are crucial for productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The draft bill is currently discussed in Congress.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the National Innovation Council represents a significant structural change in the governance of innovation policy, aiming to enhance the effectiveness and independence of innovation efforts in Chile.
reforms.source_quote: According to a draft bill currently discussed in Congress the members of the National Innovation Council would be independent (nominated by the president and ratified by the Senate)...

reforms.description: The reform of the innovation policy framework aims to strengthen links between universities and firms, moving beyond a narrow focus on R&D to support all forms of innovation in firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration between academia and industry, the reform is expected to foster innovation and improve the practical application of research, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The innovation policy framework has improved considerably over the past years and many promising projects have been launched.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to innovation policy, broadening the focus to include various forms of innovation, which is crucial for enhancing the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: In innovation policy, continue efforts to strengthen links between universities and firms and continue to move away from the narrow focus on R&D and support all forms of innovation in firms.

reforms.description: Chile's National Innovation Policy for Competitiveness outlines a strategic framework to enhance innovation across various sectors, aiming to improve productivity and economic growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering innovation, the policy is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy was put into action as part of the government's strategic initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This policy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing innovation, which is critical for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Política Nacional de Innovación para la Competitividad: Orientaciones y Plan de Acción 2009-2010.

reforms.description: Recent reforms to the innovation policy framework have been implemented, focusing on sectoral priority clusters, which will require appropriate monitoring procedures and sunset clauses for public support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening the innovation policy framework is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness in targeted sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms to the innovation policy framework have been welcomed and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the innovation policy landscape, aiming to enhance productivity and competitiveness across sectors.
reforms.source_quote: Recent reforms to the innovation policy framework are welcome but the focus on sectoral priority clusters will need to be accompanied by appropriate monitoring procedures and sunset clauses for public support.

reforms.description: The Chilean government introduced an R&D tax credit in 2008 to incentivize private sector investment in research and development, although the uptake has been limited due to a complex certification process and lack of awareness among businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private investment in R&D, which can lead to innovation and productivity improvements in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D tax credit was introduced in 2008 and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the R&D tax credit represents a significant policy shift aimed at enhancing private sector R&D investment, although its effectiveness is still being evaluated.
reforms.source_quote: Since the introduction of the R&D tax credit in 2008, 56 research centres have registered with the economic development agency and 8 R&D contracts have been certified.

reforms.description: The National Innovation Council for Competitiveness has initiated a cluster strategy to enhance competitiveness and diversify production by identifying priority sectors for public support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on sectors with growth potential, the strategy aims to foster innovation and economic diversification, which can enhance overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Innovation Council has outlined the cluster strategy in its white book.
reforms.importance_rationale: The cluster strategy represents a significant effort to align public support with market opportunities, although its long-term impact remains to be seen.
reforms.source_quote: The cluster strategy aims at enhancing competitiveness and the diversification of production.

reforms.description: The government introduced the Subvención Escolar Preferencial (SEP) subsidy in 2008, which provides financial support and follow-up for schools enrolling disadvantaged students, aiming to improve educational outcomes for the poorest children.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing resources for disadvantaged students, the SEP aims to enhance educational quality and outcomes, which can lead to a more skilled workforce and higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The SEP subsidy was introduced and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The SEP represents a significant policy shift aimed at addressing educational inequalities, with potential long-term impacts on human capital development and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Subvención Escolar Preferencial (SEP) subsidy introduced in 2008 involves financial support and follow-up for schools that enroll disadvantaged students.
